issue_title: request: add FEC decoding in Decoding section
issue_body:
for transmitters, which use FEC (forward error correction) they use a Viterbi / trellis algorithm to scramble data with additional bytes.
For these kind of Payload data, it is hard to interpret the raw payload stream with urh. The cc1101 uses the following algorithm after sync word: http://www.ti.com/lit/an/swra313/swra313.pdf
If possible, please add a FEC decoding item in the Decoding menu.

example payload data:
TX payload non-encoded: aaaaaaaa 57435743 06030100010203da05
TX payload FEC-encoded: aaaaaaaa 57435743 88c83c000c3330124cf03010b8dca35340347fe3

thx,
chris

issue_title: Move Gnuradio options to bottom
Prevent distasteful sample content
issue_body:
I'm using mixer to generate sample data for a software demo in a relatively conservative industry. As such, I don't want to be showing a demo of my software and have things like `nasty.new@xvideos.pe` (that's a real world example) show up as an email address - it makes us look immature when we're presenting our software to enterprise clients.

I realize we're not going to eliminate the possibility of suggestive content getting populated from time to time, but I see no reason to invite it.

Would you be open to removing some of the more explicit sample content, or providing a way to disable it? I'd be happy to do some of the work and submit a pull request, just wanted to ask first.

issue_title: allow a prefix setting for a leading path on all urls in tests
issue_body:
build_tests ought to take a prefix keyword arg to state the prefix of all the URL in a test suite.

For example if all the URLs are at `/foobar/resources` then `prefix=/foobar` might be useful.

The driving force behind this is being able to use the same tests to drive _live_ wsgi apps mounted in different places.

issue_title: Problem with trailing \n characters
issue_body:
Sometimes gdb return the characters \n...not as a new line but escapes the \\
e.g. \\\n not \n

An error is thrown when writing the following command:

### mi_cmd_to_write --- 1-target-select remote 192.168.122.48:1234
### gdb_mi_text --- =tsv-created,name="trace_timestamp",initial="0"\n

The \n is with the escaped \ and attempts to parse a string of length 0 within **_parse_val** thus throwing an unexpected character exception.

I've written a patch that searches for the characters at the end of each string to parse and removes them.

https://pastebin.com/gfuUiD8Z

I'm unsure where you want to issue a fix so haven't created a pull request.

issue_title: Parsing Fish shell aliases seems to be broken
issue_body:
In `~/.config/fish/conf.d/aliases.fish` I have `alias g 'git'` (a common alias, I think, for `g -> git`). This alias works, and even Fish auto-completion recognizes it and works.

I'm running `fish, version 2.6.0` with `The Fuck 3.24 using Python 3.6.3`, and just updated `~/.config/fish/functions/fuck.fish` to be:

```
function fuck -d "Correct your previous console command"
  set -l fucked_up_command $history[1]
  env TF_ALIAS=fuck PYTHONIOENCODING=utf-8 thefuck $fucked_up_command | read -l unfucked_command
  if [ "$unfucked_command" != "" ]
    eval $unfucked_command
    builtin history delete --exact --case-sensitive -- $fucked_up_command
    builtin history merge ^ /dev/null
  end
end
```

The `fuck` alias works for me, except, it's not resolving aliases!

I added a new rule to `~/.config/thefuck/rules/git_worktree_checkout.py`:

```
import re
from thefuck.specific.git import git_support

@git_support
def match(command):
    return ('checkout' in command.script
            and 'fatal:' in command.output
            and 'is already checked out at' in command.output)

@git_support
def get_new_command(command):
    directory_name = re.findall(
        r"fatal: '[^']+' is already checked out at '([^']+)'" , command.output)[0]
    return 'cd {}'.format(directory_name)
```

To fix the scenario of checking out a branch that's already checked out in another Git worktree. Using the `@git_support`, my _Git aliases_ (i.e. `co -> checkout`) are expanded properly, but Fish gives me `No fucks given` if I try to use `g` instead of `git`.

```
$ pwd
/home/andschwa/src/mesos
$ git checkout master
fatal: 'master' is already checked out at '/home/andschwa/src/mesos-master'
$ fuck
cd /home/andschwa/src/mesos-master [enter/↑/↓/ctrl+c]
$ cd ~/src/mesos
$ git co master
fatal: 'master' is already checked out at '/home/andschwa/src/mesos-master'
$ fuck
cd /home/andschwa/src/mesos-master [enter/↑/↓/ctrl+c]
$ cd ~/src/mesos
$ g checkout master
fatal: 'master' is already checked out at '/home/andschwa/src/mesos-master'
$ fuck
No fucks given
```

It _looks_ to me that this scenario is [supposed to be supported](https://github.com/nvbn/thefuck/blob/8fb5ddefb60c59f8bd90a72698c1a384f1433c5e/thefuck/shells/fish.py#L14), but I cannot get it to work. The `g` alias is in the output of `fish -ic functions`:

```
., N_, abbr, alias, cd, contains_seq, delete-or-exit, dirh, dirs, down-or-search, e, ec, edit_command_buffer, eval, export, extract, fish_clipboard_copy,
fish_clipboard_paste, fish_config, fish_default_key_bindings, fish_default_mode_prompt, fish_fallback_prompt, fish_greeting, fish_hybrid_key_bindings,
fish_indent, fish_key_reader, fish_md5, fish_mode_prompt, fish_prompt, fish_sigtrap_handler, fish_title, fish_update_completions, fish_vi_cursor,
fish_vi_key_bindings, fish_vi_mode, fuck, funced, funcsave, g, grep, help, history, hostname, isatty, la, ll, ls, man, math, nextd, nextd-or-forward-word,
open, p, package, popd, prevd, prevd-or-backward-word, prompt_hostname, prompt_pwd, psub, pushd, rbenv, rbt_post, rbt_update, realpath, rg, seq, setenv,
string, suspend, trap, type, umask, up-or-search, vared,
```

I have tried:

* updating thefuck
* switching to the Python 3.6 version of thefuck
* mucking around with the `THEFUCK_OVERRIDDEN_ALIASES` but learned from another issue that is for the scenario of `git` being aliased to something else (e.g. `hub`)
* restarting my shells etc. entirely
* ensuring `fish` is my login shell with `chsh`

Issue #479 seems to be the same problem, but it went away with a newer version of Python(?) I guess.

issue_title: using a config file from the current directory is insecure
issue_body:
Especially since the config file allows arbitrary code to be loaded, using one from whatever directory the user happens to be in when the keyring module runs is insecure.  
There doesn't seem to be anything to prevent an unscrupulous person from creating a config file that loads a module that reveals the authentication information.
The config file from the user's home directory should be loaded first, and the current directory's config file loaded only if explicitly enabled in the home directory config file.
Also, the config files should not be used if they are owned by anyone other that the current user, or if they are writable by anyone else.

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/kang/python-keyring-lib/issue/22
- Originally reported by: [erh](http://bitbucket.org/erh)
- Originally created at: 2010-06-22T23:24:05.638

issue_title: Add a way to run a tester on multiple circuit implementations w the same interface
issue_body:
Often we want to run a tester on multiple implementations of the "same circuit". For example if we have magma circuits, `foo_impl1` and `foo_impl2` where `foo_impl1.interface == `foo_impl2.interface`, we want to create a tester, build test vectors and then run the tests against each implementation. Right now we might do `tester = fault.Tester(foo_impl)` and then change the circuit later (i.e. `tester.circuit = foo_impl2`) to test the other implementation.

We should build a mechanism where we pass in an interface and then can run the tests against any circuit of that interface.

issue_title: text_from_points affects layout even when no points are provided
issue_body:
Repro:

```python
from adjustText import adjust_text
import numpy as np
from matplotlib import pyplot as plt

np.random.seed(1337)
n = 50
X1, Y1 = np.random.rand(n), .4 + np.random.rand(n)/5
X2, Y2 = .4+np.random.rand(n)/5, np.random.rand(n)

texts = [plt.text(x, y, '1', color='b') for x, y in zip(X1, Y1)]\
  + [plt.text(x, y, '2', color='r') for x, y in zip(X2, Y2)]

adjust_text(texts,
  #np.concatenate([X1, X2]), np.concatenate([Y1, Y2]),
  text_from_points=False,
)

plt.show()
```

When `text_from_points` is set to True, the annotations are separated perfectly. But setting it to False causes a number of text markers to overlap. (However, the result is still better than without calling `adjust_text` at all).

Since, in this example, I'm only passing in texts, and not points, I would think that the value of `text_from_points` should make no difference.

issue_title: Use atexit module to redraw cursor on program exit
issue_body:
README states: 

> Note that the cursor will stay hidden until you call cursor.show() — even after exiting your python script!
> An alternative is using the HiddenCursor() class in conjunction with Python's with statement. This will make sure that the cursor is shown again after running your code, even if exceptions are raised.

As another easy alternative, you might consider using the Python [atexit](https://docs.python.org/2/library/atexit.html) module (or just mentioning the possibility in the README  for that matter). Could work as follows:

```python
>>> import cursor
>>> import atexit
>>> atexit.register(cursor.show)
<function show at 0x7f6ebbf7b668>
>>> cursor.hide()
>>> print("hello world!")
hello world!
>>> exit()
```

issue_title: Browsing is SLOW on Raspi
issue_body:
When browsing on Raspberry Pi, a single `ListChildren` request may take up to 25s.
Nothing like this can be observed when running on Ubuntu (64 bit). Additionally, the 25s occur quite often, so this looks suspiciously like an internal timeout. Results are as expected, and no error occurs.

Some quick prototyping shows that this doesn't seem to occur when calling `ListChildren` asynchronously. Probably that's why this cannot be observed when searching (which usually finishes well below 1s), but can be observed in directory/artist/album lookups.

Thias has to be investigated further, but currently it looks like performing all operations asynchronously seems to be the way to go (which also has other benefits, e.g. when doing incremental/batch browse/search).

issue_title: PyPI wheel packages for Linux
issue_body:
With PIP 8.1 it is possible to distribute wheel packages for Linux. From release notes:
> Implement PEP 513, which adds support for the manylinux1 platform tag, allowing carefully compiled binary wheels to be installed on compatible Linux platforms.

PEP 513: https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0513/

Wheel packages preserve files permissions so there is no need to run setup.py postinstall hook to chmod files using sudo. Looks like the only thing to make it work out of the box is to remove the libudev symlink, which will be possible after update to Chrome 47.

Related issues:
#148 - remove libudev symlink - this should make Linux wheel package to work out of the box on Debian/Ubuntu
#144 closed - cefpython3 package on PyPI - see for comments
#145 closed - Linux installers dependencies and libudev

issue_title: Question - Dropping Postgresql / SQLAlchemy tables which have partitoned data
issue_body:
I'm working on a data model, and in the process of creating it I'm finding that if partitions are created and I try to use SQLAlchemy's `MetaData.drop_all()` method with Postgresql 9.4 the table is not dropped due to other tables inheriting my parent table.

My motivation for this question is to be able to fully automate my unit / integration tests. I'd like to be able to drop my project on a clean server with no outside dependencies except for what I've declared.

I've been trying to go over the SQLAlchemy docs to see if there's an easy way to overload the 'DROP TABLE' clause to use Postgresql's 'CASCADING' option to no avail.

Additionally, I'd like to be able to clean up my data model to drop the functions which architect installs to partition the table's data. 

My current solution is to do these tasks with a SQLAlchemy event monitor for 'before_drop' where I run some queries against the information_schema. Unfortunately this must be done on a table-by-table basis so I was wondering if there's a cleaner way to perform these tasks.

issue_title: release 0.10.0
issue_body:
This will include the complete protocol rewrite, whose main user-visible feature is the ability for me to bounce the relay server while a client is waiting for their peer to connect, without losing the connection (and burning that wormhole code). The internal API is not yet stable, but I'm much happier with this one than what was in 0.9.x .

Our release is blocked on landing #171, which makes sure that the client bails if the first attempt to connect to the relay has failed. This is what 0.9.x did too, but the new state machines in trunk would silently attempt to reconnect forever, which doesn't seem like a great user experience. We can't land #171 until Twisted-17.5 is released, since it depends upon a new `twisted.application.internet.clientService` API that is in Twisted trunk but not yet in a release.

We also want #170, but that should be trivial.

issue_title: `autoBalance = 1` does not balance
issue_body:
I don't have access to ArcMap, but a customer told me that the shapefile I made with `pyshp` had a mismatched count of shapes and records. If `len(w._shapes)` and `len(w.records)` are useful metrics before writing, I think I found a bug:

```python
import shapefile
w = shapefile.Writer()
w.shapetype = shapefile.POLYLINE
w.autoBalance = 1
w.field('myfield', 'C', 10, 0)

issue_title: Trusted Advisor returning limits as "Unlimited"
issue_body:
This morning for the first time, I saw this for one of our accounts in us-east-1, using awslimitchecker 0.4.4:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "./check_aws_limits.py", line 289, in <module>
    checker.run(error_on_warning=args.error_on_warning, verbose=args.verbose)
  File "./check_aws_limits.py", line 249, in run
    warnings, criticals = self.run_for_regions(regions, verbose=verbose)
  File "./check_aws_limits.py", line 230, in run_for_regions
    w, c = self.run_for_region(region=region, verbose=verbose)
  File "./check_aws_limits.py", line 201, in run_for_region
    return self.check_limits(region, verbose=verbose)
  File "./check_aws_limits.py", line 141, in check_limits
    checker.check_thresholds()
  File "/home/ec2-user/jenkins/workspace/ReleaseEngineering/awslimitchecker/awslimitchecker_prod/lib/python2.7/site-packages/awslimitchecker/checker.py", line 413, in check_thresholds
    self.ta.update_limits()
  File "/home/ec2-user/jenkins/workspace/ReleaseEngineering/awslimitchecker/awslimitchecker_prod/lib/python2.7/site-packages/awslimitchecker/trustedadvisor.py", line 117, in update_limits
    ta_results = self._poll()
  File "/home/ec2-user/jenkins/workspace/ReleaseEngineering/awslimitchecker/awslimitchecker_prod/lib/python2.7/site-packages/awslimitchecker/trustedadvisor.py", line 162, in _poll
    res[data['Service']][data['Limit Name']] = int(data['Limit Amount'])
ValueError: invalid literal for int() with base 10: 'Unlimited'
```

Sure enough, as of this morning, the Trusted Advisor check for EC2 service, "On-Demand instances - t2.micro" in us-west-2 has started returning a value of "Unlimited" instead of something that can be parsed as an integer.

I'll need to:

1. modify the TrustedAdvisor code to handle this. And ideally modify the TA code so anything else that's a non-integer (since apparently we can't count on "Limit Amount" actually being a numeric amount, because AWS's API specifications are really just taken as suggestions) just triggers an error-level log message.
2. Figure out how to represent "unlimited" internally. Do we set something to e.g. MAXINT, use a magic value like -1, just stop looking at the limit, or - probably most likely - set an attribute on the AWSLimit object that just stops it from being alerted on.

``` python

    def get_app(self, app=None):

issue_title: Add ability to handle roles and give only "bloggers" permission to edit!
Django Admin scripts don't included in the admin form
issue_body:
I have a mptt model with SlugField
```
# @python_2_unicode_compatible
class Category(MPTTModel):
    name = models.CharField(
        max_length=255)

    slug = models.SlugField()

    parent = TreeForeignKey(
        to='self',
        related_name='children',
        null=True, blank=True,
        db_index=True)

    class Meta:
        unique_together = [
            ('parent', 'name')
        ]

    class MPTTMeta:
        order_insertion_by = ['name']

    def __str__(self):
        return '{}'.format(self.name)
```
and DjangoMpttAdmin admin with a prepopulated field.
```
class CategoryAdmin(DjangoMpttAdmin):
    prepopulated_fields = {"slug": ("name",)}
```

Then I open /admin/app/category/add page, the slug field prepopulation doesn't work. I see an error message in the Javascript console:
```
Uncaught TypeError: $ is not a function
```

Django embeds a couple of scripts on the admin form page:
```
(function($) {
...
})(django.jQuery);
```
but django.jQuery doesn't defined beacuse jquery.init.js doesn't included into the page.

I tried to fix this with workaround:
```
class CategoryAdmin(DjangoMpttAdmin):
    prepopulated_fields = {"slug": ("name",)}

    @property
    def media(self):
        media = super(DjangoMpttAdmin, self).media
        extra = '' if settings.DEBUG else '.min'
        js = [
            'core.js',
            'vendor/jquery/jquery%s.js' % extra,
            'jquery.init.js',
            'admin/RelatedObjectLookups.js',
            'actions%s.js' % extra,
            'urlify.js',
            'prepopulate%s.js' % extra,
            'vendor/xregexp/xregexp.min.js',
        ]
        media.add_js(['admin/js/%s' % url for url in js])
        return media
```
( https://github.com/django/django/blob/master/django/contrib/admin/options.py#L568-L577 )

But then I got the `Uncaught TypeError: jQuery is not a function` from the `django_mptt_admin.js` because `jquery.init.js` install jQuery with a `noconflict` mode. 

I think, standard Django Admin scripts should be included anyway because they are used in the standard field widgets. 
If django_mptt_admin.js uses jQuery from Django Admin, it should call it as `django.jQuery`, not `jQuery`.

issue_title: default value ignored when infer_missing=True
issue_body:
When infer_missing is set to True, it by-pass default argument.  
Consider the following code :

```
import json
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from dataclasses_json import DataClassJsonMixin

@dataclass()
class MyObject(DataClassJsonMixin):
    id: str
    name: str
    category: str = field(default="my_object")


s1 = MyObject(id="12345678-5ef8-49d5-be8b-44fcb6907c16", name="test_me")
print(json.dumps(s1.__dict__))

s2 = MyObject.from_json('{"id": "12345678-5ef8-49d5-be8b-44fcb6907c16", "name": "test_me"}', infer_missing=True)
print(s2.to_json())
```

Result is :
```
{"id": "12345678-5ef8-49d5-be8b-44fcb6907c16", "name": "test_me", "category": "my_object"}
{"id": "12345678-5ef8-49d5-be8b-44fcb6907c16", "name": "test_me", "category": null}
```

The `category` is replaced by `null`, not by the default value `my_object`

issue_title: campaign.actions.schedule raises TypeError
issue_body:
At least under Python 3.5 (have not tested other versions), the ``schedule`` function raises a ``TypeError: utcoffset() takes exactly one argument (0 given)``

According to the documentation, and also in the ``pytz`` implementation of ``tzinfo`` (I have tested with ``timezone.utc`` and ``pytz.utc``, both raise that Error), the utcoffset function wants the datetime as a parameter:
https://docs.python.org/3.5/library/datetime.html#datetime.tzinfo.utcoffset

You should just use ``datetime.utcoffset()``:
https://docs.python.org/3.5/library/datetime.html#datetime.datetime.utcoffset

issue_title: DBAdminClearableFileInput not showing files with russian letters in name
undocumented feature: defining a DjangoObjectType as baseType for DjangoListObjectType or DjangoSerializerType
issue_body:
Hi,
first of all: awesome package!
I don't really have an issue to report, but a feature that should be documented and was hard for me to find.
My problem was that I intended to use DjangoSerializerType for common queries and mutations. Everything worked fine, apart from including property functions from the django model. I had to define a baseType like this, which worked fine:

```
class InstrumentType(DjangoObjectType):
    is_at = graphene.Field(LocationType.object_type(), source='is_at')
    class Meta:
        model = Instrument
```

But then I couldn't use the nice features of DjangoSerializerType or DjangoListObjectType. Then I searched for a long time how to pass that InstrumentType to e.g. a DjangoSerializerType. Finally I found out that you use this line in the types.py to get an already defined baseType from the registry:
```
output_type = registry.get_type_for_model(model)
if not output_type:
     output_type = factory_type('output', DjangoObjectType, **factory_kwargs)
```

But this depends on the order of the type definitions in the source code and is real implicit behaviour.
After I found that out, I could use my InstrumentSerializerType with correct custom baseType just like this:

```
class InstrumentSerializerType(DjangoSerializerType):
    class Meta:
        serializer_class = InstrumentSerializer
```

I really suggest you document that behaviour, since it's a powerful feature and can also have unexpected negative effects if you don't know about. 

Thanks for the nice work,
Greets,
Bene

issue_title: Wrongly recognized Django url pattern
issue_body:
Below description is copied from [my stackoverflow post][1].

  [1]: http://stackoverflow.com/questions/31797515/swagger-ui-wrongly-recognized-django-url-pattern

## Intorudction ##

I've implemented rest API using `django-rest-framework`.

I've decided to implement batch operations, where ids were neccessary, in two ways:

 1. By sending ids in url, separated with '/':

        url(r'^tubes/(?P<ids>(([0-9]+/)+))$', manage_tubes, name='manage_tubes'),
    Example request:
    >http://127.0.0.1:8000/tubes/0/1/10/


 2. By sending ids in POST body as a json array:

        url(r'^tubes/(?P<operation>(create|delete|get))/$', manage_tubes_json, name='manage_tubes_json'),
    Example request:
    >http://127.0.0.1:8000/tubes/get

    with following body:
    >[0, 1, 10]

This works well, but I've problem with `django-rest-swagger`. Actually with `swagger UI`.

## Problem ##

Swagger didn't recognized my url patterns very well...

It sees urls as follows:

  1. >/tubes/{ids}+))

issue_title: Support numpy in the APIs
issue_body:
Right now we take or return Python lists from functions where the underlying driver API takes or returns an array. Should we use numpy in our APIs?

Here's a performance comparison on NI-DMM fetch(). We use simulation so the data is probably getting generated as we fetch.

1. List: After calling the library, samples are copied onto a Python list.
2. numpy: Uses a hacked version of the Python bindings to pass a numpy array straight to the library, which is returned.
3. LabVIEW (LV): Allocation happens in the LabVIEW VI. Notably, this zeroes all the data before passing to the library.

|              | List (s) | numpy (s) | LV (s) |
|-------------:|----------|-----------|--------|
| 1M samples   | 0.2      | 0.04      | 0.04   |
| 10M samples  | 2.2      | 0.4       | 0.41   |
| 100M samples | 23       | 3.8       | 4.1    |

Questions

* Should we use numpy at all?
  * Looks like probably yes
* Should the API have methods for both numpy as well as Python list? Or numpy only?
* Do we need to somehow make the numpy dependency optional?
* Do we allocate the numpy buffer or require the user to allocate?
  * Make it optional and allocate if the user doesn't?

issue_title: BoxSession._renew_session does not set previously refreshed tokens
issue_body:
I am using `CooperativelyManagedOAuth2` so that I can use the same oauth tokens for multiple clients but it seems like refreshed tokens do not get updated in the session if the tokens were refreshed by another client. I have two instances of the oauth class using the same tokens and two clients using one of the instances.

For example, say there are two clients initialized with the same tokens but different `CooperativelyManagedOAuth2` instances. The first client makes a request, sees the access token is expired and refreshes the tokens. The tokens in the first client's oauth instance are updated with the new tokens. The second client then makes a request, sees it's access token is expired and tries to refresh the token. The token is already refreshed so it does not try to refresh them again but then does not replace the old tokens so when client two retries the request it will raise a `BoxAPIException`.

issue_title: the GRU forward calculation time test
issue_body:
Hello everyone, i write a GRU unit myself, because my training is very slow, so i want to test how long it will take to calculate one time GRU, the forward function is as following:

```
def add_input(self, x_i, x_m=None):

        z_i = logistic(self.W_z_xh * x_i + self.U_z_xh * self.h_im1 + self.b_z_xh)

        r_i = logistic(self.W_r_xh * x_i + self.U_r_xh * self.h_im1 + self.b_r_xh)

        h_i_below = self.activation(self.W_h_xh * x_i + 
                    self.U_h_xh * cmult(r_i, self.h_im1) + self.b_h_xh)

        h_i = cmult((1 - z_i), self.h_im1) + cmult(z_i, h_i_below)

        if x_m is not None:
            batch_size = x_m.shape[0]
            m_matrix = np.reshape(np.tile(x_m, (1, self.output_dims)), \
                                  (batch_size * self.output_dims))
            x_m_expr = reshape(inputVector(m_matrix), 
                                  (self.output_dims,), batch_size)
            _1m_x_m_expr = reshape(inputVector(1.-m_matrix), 
                                  (self.output_dims,), batch_size)
            h_i = cmult(x_m_expr, h_i) + cmult(_1m_x_m_expr, self.h_im1)
```

the weight matrix size are: 

```
    GRU_Enc_fwd_1_W_z   : (512, 256)
    GRU_Enc_fwd_1_U_z   : (512, 512)
    GRU_Enc_fwd_1_b_z   : (512,)
    GRU_Enc_fwd_1_W_r   : (512, 256)
    GRU_Enc_fwd_1_U_r   : (512, 512)
    GRU_Enc_fwd_1_b_r   : (512,)
    GRU_Enc_fwd_1_W_h   : (512, 256)
    GRU_Enc_fwd_1_U_h   : (512, 512)
    GRU_Enc_fwd_1_b_h   : (512,)
```

issue_title: Add `apilib` module
`clilib` allow custom names for sub-commands passed to ArgumentParser
issue_body: Right now, subcommand names for `clilib.ArgumentParser` can only be function names. Thus, reserved words like `class` are impossible and names like `list` would cause shadowing of built-ins where the function is defined. So there should be a way to register callables as commands under custom names.

We run around 30 applications, which have around 10 releases each on average per day. After 4 to 5 days, inodes become 100% (disk usage is still 45%).

We resolve this by

root@my-local# deisctl ssh deis

issue_title: [Notice] Bower version mismatch
issue_body:
When you use this package with bower you get this:

```
Installing bower components...
bower bootstrap-sass-official#>=3.1.1           cached git://github.com/twbs/bootstrap-sass.git#3.3.3
bower bootstrap-sass-official#>=3.1.1         validate 3.3.3 against git://github.com/twbs/bootstrap-sass.git#>=3.1.1
bower bootstrap-sass-official#>=3.1.1              new version for git://github.com/twbs/bootstrap-sass.git#>=3.1.1
bower bootstrap-sass-official#>=3.1.1          resolve git://github.com/twbs/bootstrap-sass.git#>=3.1.1
bower bootstrap-sass-official#>=3.1.1         download https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap-sass/archive/v3.3.4.tar.gz
bower bootstrap-sass-official#>=3.1.1          extract archive.tar.gz
bower bootstrap-sass-official#>=3.1.1         mismatch Version declared in the json (3.3.3) is different than the resolved one (3.3.4)
bower bootstrap-sass-official#>=3.1.1         resolved git://github.com/twbs/bootstrap-sass.git#3.3.4
bower bootstrap-sass-official#>=3.1.1          install bootstrap-sass-official#3.3.4
```

This is because v3.3.4 tag in this repo does not incude commit that bupms to v3.3.4 in bower.json (https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap-sass/commit/d047ceac00b1a03a8931e96a281d7eaf9a8f8979). 

So, that makes some noise during installation, but really no harm.

Is there a way to fix this without introducing a new tag/version?

issue_title: Target.is_class should use inspect.is_class
issue_body:
Is there any reason why `Target.is_class` doesn't invoke the inspect module's is_class method? The current implementation can break if the doubled object overrides `__eq__`. 

This revealed a bug in my `__eq__` override (since I should immediately return False if the two objects aren't the same instance), but it seems cleaner to just inspect the object directly instead of invoking `__eq__`.

issue_title: Inconsistent error thrown when naming a missing blob using a pattern
issue_body:
If I try to delete a blob with a pattern that doesn't match anything:

    az storage blob copy start-batch --destination-container="foo" --source-container "bar" \
                                     --pattern "foo/*"

or even

    az storage blob copy start-batch ... --pattern "foo*"

I get an expected empty list of copied paths.  However, if there is no
`*` in the pattern I get an error:

    az storage blob copy start-batch ... --pattern "foo"
    → Failed to copy blob foo to container deleted.

This is inconsistent: I expected this case to also produce an empty list.

It is particularly bad when viewing storage as a hierarchy of
directories and files: in such a case, to copy `foo`, I need to do two
copies:

    az storage blob copy start-batch ... --pattern "foo"
    az storage blob copy start-batch ... --pattern "foo/*"

because the union of both cannot be done in a single glob (and using
`foo*` will also remove, for example `foo1`, which is wrong).

issue_title: Single-version API clients are potentially not forward-compatible
issue_body:
I keep forgetting to ask about this: single-version API clients are not being generated with the same patterns as multi-version APIs. The most notable omissions are the `api_version` and `profile` args on the main client `__init__`; this prevents the current API version from being "pinned" by a caller, so a future SDK upgrade that adds a new (potentially incompatible) version could break it.

Was this an oversight, or is there a reason not to follow the multi-version pattern on all clients/models?

issue_title: Regression in conda 4.3.33 regarding build features
issue_body:
It seems that `conda 4.3.33` installs packages that have a build feature when installing any package that has the same build feature.

From a fresh miniconda, with conda pinned at `4.3.32`:

```sh
scorlay@mbp ~]$ conda install cryptopp -c QuantStack -c conda-forge
Fetching package metadata ...............
Solving package specifications: .

Package plan for installation in environment /Users/scorlay/miniconda3:

The following NEW packages will be INSTALLED:

    cling-patches: 1-0           QuantStack
    cryptopp:      5.6.5-cling_0 QuantStack [cling]
```

With `conda 4.3.33`

```sh
[scorlay@mbp ~]$ conda install cryptopp -c QuantStack -c conda-forge
Fetching package metadata ...............
Solving package specifications: .

Package plan for installation in environment /Users/scorlay/miniconda3:

The following NEW packages will be INSTALLED:

    clangdev:      5.0.0-cling_1 QuantStack  [cling]
    cling-patches: 1-0           QuantStack 
    cryptopp:      5.6.5-cling_0 QuantStack  [cling]
    icu:           58.2-0        conda-forge
    libiconv:      1.15-0        conda-forge
    libxml2:       2.9.7-0       conda-forge
    llvmdev:       5.0.0-cling_1 QuantStack  [cling]
```

The only package tracking the `cling` build feature is `cling-patches`.

Notice in the second case that `clangdev` gets installed while it is not a dependency.

issue_title: `conda reload` command
issue_body:
I often find myself needing to re-load the current environment, using `source activate <env>`, for example after rebuilding and re-installing a package that I'm working on. It would be helpful to have an env-agnostic command that could be called from a rebuild script, like this:

```
conda build <recipe>
conda uninstall <package>
conda install <package> --use-local
conda reload
```

where `conda reload` basically does `source activate <current-env>`, without having to specify the env name.

issue_title: [Bug] conda removes folders from working directory when they share name with environment
issue_body:
conda removes folders from working directory when they share their name with the environment that you want to delete. You can find a minimal reproduction at the tagged commit linked here:

https://github.com/Korijn/condabug/tree/conda-env-remove

# Steps to reproduce

Run the following command from the root of the repository:

```batch
> conda env create
> conda remove -n hw --all -y
```

*Conda has now deleted `hw` source folder!*

Since `conda env remove`'s behavior was redirect to the same code in a fairly recent release, you can also reproduce the bug like this:

```batch
> conda env create
> conda env remove -n hw -y
```

issue_title: Recent conda changes breaking Travis
issue_body:
Not 100% sure that this is a conda issue, but it looks like changes in .27 are breaking Travis builds that 
use extension modules. 

In particular, the compiler is identified as 
```
compiler: x86_64-conda_cos6-linux-gnu-gcc -pthread -Wno-unused-result -Wsign-compare -DNDEBUG -fwrapv -O2 -Wall -Wstrict-prototypes -march=nocona -mtune=haswell -ftree-vectorize -fPIC -fstack-protector-strong -fno-plt -O2 -pipe -march=nocona -mtune=haswell -ftree-vectorize -fPIC -fstack-protector-strong -fno-plt -O2 -pipe -fPIC
```

Not sure why `conda` would be in the compiler.  

And example of a failed build can be see https://travis-ci.org/statsmodels/statsmodels/builds/280537305

issue_title: Binary nose package not properly registering the distutils entry point.
issue_body:
Currently, the ``nose-1.3.4-py27_0`` package contains ``lib/python2.7/site-packages/nose-1.3.4-py2.7.egg-info`` rather than the directory ``lib/python2.7/site-packages/nose-1.3.4.dist-info`` installed by ``pip install nose``.  This somehow does not properly register the ``distutils`` ``nosetest`` entry-point, breaking ``python setup.py nosetests`` as described in [this nose issue](https://github.com/nose-devs/nose/issues/873).

I'm not sure exactly what the problem is, but it would probably be solved by rebuilding the current package with a recent version of ``pip`` or ``setuptools``.

P.S. If this is not the correct place for submitting broken package issues, please let me know where the should go instead as it is not clear from the documentation.

issue_title: conda info to report LD_LIBRARY_PATH or DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH
issue_body: We need to try and help people help themselves and to head off common causes of Anaconda/Conda failure, such as LD_LIBRARY_PATH and DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH.  Longer term a `conda debug` command might be useful (similarly a GUI version in Navigator), but in the short term it would probably go a long way to helping us if we reported `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` or `DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH` with the output of `conda info`.

issue_title: connection_timeout not honoured?
issue_body:
I ran into a problem with a docker compose setup with a python app and a neo4j 3.3 server. The app container can start up before the Neo server is ready. 
I get this exception:
```
neo4j.exceptions.ServiceUnavailable: Cannot acquire connection to Address(host='neo', port=7687)
```
So I wanted to use the connection_timeout to a largish number (like 10) to allow for this. 
```
    driver = GraphDatabase.driver( "bolt://neo:7687", connection_timeout=10)
```
However it seems it doesn't do anything. I still get the exception and the driver call does not take noticeably longer. 
If I manually restart the app everything is OK.

Also I noticed that you can pass any config parameter without warning. It seems there is no checking for valid parameters.


Using python driver 1.5.2

issue_title: Unable to run `nosetests` inside a `virtualenv` because of missing dependencies in requirements.txt
issue_body:
To enable running `nosetests` within virtual environments, we need to add new dependencies to requirements.txt. Here is the list:

* nose==1.3.1
* coverage==4.0.3 (if you want to generate coverage reports)

This would ensure that running `nosetests` will run nose from the current virtualenv.

However, I'm not sure if we should have dev dependencies within `requirements.txt`. I can see `mock==1.3.0` in there which does not seem appropriate. I think we should have a separate file called `test-requirements.txt` that contains such secondary requirements[1].

[1] http://nvie.com/posts/pin-your-packages/

issue_title: Remove boilerplate code for inplace operations in Factors, TabularCPDs.
issue_body:
Currently, in both ```Factor``` as well as in ```TabularCPD``` class, methods like ```normalize```, ```reduce```, ```marginalize``` have a ```inplace``` as an input argument. But the way that is implemented in the methods is:

```
if inplace:
    factor = self
else:
    factor = Factor(.., ..., ...)
```

and this code is repeated in lot of the places. Replace the boilerplate code with something more clean and elegant.

issue_title: Change Pulp-Smash Code layout to fit Pulp2 and Pulp3 needs.
issue_body:
Code related to Pulp3 will be added to Pulp-Smash soon.

Based on that it does make sense to modify pulp-smash current layout to fit this need. Below, a draft solution of future layout .

```
pulp_smash/
├── constants.py
├── pulp2
│   ├── constants.py
│   └── tests
└── pulp3
    ├── constants.py
    └── tests
```

Every project will have its own dir, and it is related files. All the other files there a common will be in a level above.  For instance, the constant files for Pulp3 will have all related constants of Pulp3. Constants shared by both projects, for instance, pulp-fixtures will be in another constant file, a level above.

issue_title: Creation of db files when import qcodes
issue_body:
It's not a bug I guess, but I think its still unwanted. 

### Steps to reproduce
Set any directory to the current working directory
Execute a python script/program that just imports the qcodes module

### Expected behaviour
No side-effects of the file system

### Actual behaviour
An experiment.db file is created in the current working directory. 

I think it's better if measurement databases are only created when we actually call a function that initialized an experiment. 
I use the qcodes module to load my experimental data from a lot of different working directories, like folders where I work on papers, during the analyses, etc...

issue_title: Adding placeholder in german causes english to be created
issue_body:
Hello,

When I create an article in german and go to it's detail page, add a text plugin and save, this triggers an insert in the translations  table for english and thus creates the english translation without a title/content without me asking it to do so.

The problem lies [here](https://github.com/aldryn/aldryn-newsblog/blob/master/aldryn_newsblog/models.py#L516), in here we try to save the search_data when a plugin is added to article's content placeholder but it never checks the plugin's language and so it gets the article which in my case comes back in english and saves which causes the english translation to be created.

Seems like an easy fix, here just need to use instance.language to make sure the correct translation is updated.

Here's a video of the issue https://www.dropbox.com/s/yms4s7gdokuhv68/newsblog-pl-issue.webm?dl=0

issue_title: Parallel Unrolling of Stacked Recurrent Neural Networks
issue_body:
Has anyone tried to construct Deep RNNs which unroll stacked RNNs in parallel?

That is, if there are 3 stacked RNNs (`RNN1`, `RNN2` & `RNN3`), and suppose the input has 5 timesteps (let's ignore batch size for now) most implementations I know of first run 5 recurrent steps fully on `RNN1` and then run 5 steps on `RNN2` and then 5 steps on `RNN3` one by one by one. What I'm proposing here is to run all three RNNs in parallel, but each RNN lags the RNN below it by a timestep. That is, when `RNN1` is in it's 3rd recurrent step, `RNN2` is in its 2nd and `RNN3` is in its 1st step and all 3 RNNs are executed in parallel and they go to the next time step together.

Obvious benefit is speed and device utilization (which is done mostly by increasing batch size elsewhere). Normal Deep RNNs should take `N * T` steps if there are `N` stacked RNNs and `T` timesteps. This proposed method should reduce it to `N + T` steps. This obviously can be extended to all forms of RNNs - LSTMs and GRUs.

I'll be trying to implement this soon. I'll be using **MXNET's ND ARRAY** backend mainly because this should be easy to do on top of your _lazy evaluation_ scheme. I'm not sure if the MXNET engine deduces that this optimization is possible from the dependency graph and implicitly use this method even for normally programmed Deep RNNs. Does it do this automatically? Has this idea been done/considered before? Any inputs regarding any aspect of this is welcome.

issue_title: Proposal: expose ABCs in `backports_abc` namespace
issue_body:
I currently use the following conditional import (in Tornado) to get the generator type for `isinstance()` checks:

```
try:
    from collections.abc import Generator as GeneratorType  # py35+
except ImportError:
    from types import GeneratorType
```

This doesn't work on py27 with `backports_abc`, since the ABCs live in `collections` instead of `collections.abc`. I need to add another attempt to the conditional (`from collections import Generator as GeneratorType`), but since the only time this import would ever succeed is when `backports_abc` is present, I'd rather just do `from backports_abc import Generator`. 

If this pattern were adopted by all users of `backports_abc`, the `patch()` function would become unnecessary (but could still be useful on py3.x, where it installs the ABCs where a backport-unaware caller would expect to find them).

Specifically, I would do something like this in `backports_abc.py` for each patchable type:
```
try:
    Generator = _collections_abc.Generator
except AttributeError:
    Generator = mk_gen()
```

issue_title: Improve the naming of things?
issue_body:
I think the fast.ai lessons are great and I like the idea of the fastai library but the (idiosyncratic) naming style of the library makes my brain hurt -- and I believe it will hurt adoption of this library.

Some examples: can you tell what these names mean?

- `m`
- `sz`
- `bs`
- `lrs`
- `wds`
- `tfms`

It's hard to tell what they are without context but even _in context_ it's often not obvious what these things mean. I called it an idiosyncratic naming style because this is how Jeremy likes to name the variables in his notebooks during the lessons, but they are not standard abbreviations and they're not obvious (at least not to me).

I suggest using terms that are fully written out:

- `model`
- `input_size` or `image_size` (or even `img_size`)
- `batch_size`
- `learning_rates`
- `weight_decays`
- `transforms`

It's not just the above terms; abbreviations and two-letter parameter names are used throughout the codebase. If I see a parameter `sz` it doesn't immediately read as `size` to me, and even if it does, it still doesn't tell me _what_ it is the size of.

In my own library, I have a function `plot_most_confident_incorrect_by_recall()`. Long name but it's pretty obvious what it does (it plots the examples that were classified with high confidence but that were incorrect according to the recall metric).

API design is user interface design. If this library is intended to make deep learning easy (or uncool  again :smile:) then in my opinion the API could be made a lot more frictionless.

Thanks!

issue_title: Produce more useful error on endpoints where autoactivation fails to activate
issue_body:
Right now, the error output is not prescriptive, and just notes that an endpoint needs to be activated.
Example from Steve:

``` shell
$ globus transfer ls --endpoint-id 9d6d994a-6d04-11e5-ba46-22000b92c6ec
Globus CLI Error: A Transfer API Error Occurred.
HTTP status:      400
request_id:       9sHmaySoX
code:             ClientError.ActivationRequired
message:          The endpoint '9d6d994a-6d04-11e5-ba46-22000b92c6ec' is not activated
```

This is actually the LS call returning the `ActivationRequired` error, not a processed activation requirements doc.

What we really need to do is this: autoactivate any endpoint that we see, and capture any activation requirements document that we get back.
If the response has a success code, proceed as usual.
However, if we get `NotActivated`, we need to parse and present the `ActivationRequirements`.
I think in that case, we should exit with status 1 -- the command failed, after all.

As for the error presentation, it depends a bit on the required activation type.
For web activation, we just need to show the activation link, that's pretty easy.
For CLI activation, we need some text that clarifies that we mean the hosted CLI.
Possibly something like this:

``` shell
$ globus transfer ls --endpoint-id 9d6d994a-6d04-11e5-ba46-22000b92c6ec
Globus CLI Error: A Globus Endpoint Requires Activation
code:             ClientError.ActivationRequired
message:          The endpoint '9d6d994a-6d04-11e5-ba46-22000b92c6ec' is not activated

To activate 9d6d994a-6d04-11e5-ba46-22000b92c6ec , you must use the Hosted Globus CLI.
Follow this guide to connect <link>
Then run
  endpoint-activate 9d6d994a-6d04-11e5-ba46-22000b92c6ec
```

Note that I omit some of the HTTP detail (it's a success there, after all), but still want the `ClientError.ActivationRequired` code noted.

This may have some unforeseen impact on #21, so we need to think about that.

Ideally, the SDK would handle parsing the activation requirements document a bit, and we could inspect an `activation_type` property on it to see CLI vs. Web activation.

issue_title: `globus transfer async-transfer` and `globus-transfer async-delete` should print out the Task ID in text mode
issue_body:
The textual output from the task submission commands doesn't include the Task ID -- just the "message" field back from the API.

This is a usability problem, as immediately after submission, starting something like a `globus transfer task wait` requires doing a task listing.
The JSON output includes all of the fields, so this isn't a problem for scripting, but rather for interactive use.

The text output format should probably be something like this:
```shell
$ globus transfer async-transfer ...
Message: The transfer has been accepted and a task has been created and queued for execution
Task ID:    aaaaa-bbbbb-ccccc-ddddd
```

That's just for consistency with the various other parts of the CLI that output multi-field text info in a non-columnar format.
The alternative is to ditch that format and go with
```
$ globus transfer async-transfer ...
The transfer has been accepted and a task has been created and queued for execution
Task ID:    aaaaa-bbbbb-ccccc-ddddd
```

To me, those two are indistinguishable for a user, so we should just go with the one that matches other existing output.

issue_title: Running mean-variance during first batches
issue_body:
The implementation of the running mean and variance has the following `update` method which was inspired by [this](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Algorithms_for_calculating_variance#Parallel_algorithm):
https://github.com/openai/baselines/blob/b5be53dc928bc19c39bce2a3f8a4e7dd0374f1dd/baselines/common/vec_env/vec_normalize.py#L69
While this works once the `self.count` is not zero as for the very first batch and then large enough compared to `batch_count` for the first number of batches the resulting reward in the `VecNormalize` peaks unnaturally high compared to later updates. Is this behaviour neglectable for the training of an agent or would it make sense to modify the `update` method and apply a different algorithm while `self.count` is not way larger then `batch_count`.

issue_title: Missing norm argument in several numpy interface fft methods
issue_body:
In the [pyfftw numpy interfaces module](https://github.com/pyFFTW/pyFFTW/blob/master/pyfftw/interfaces/numpy_fft.py), some of the fft methods do not have a `norm` argument. For instance: 
```
def fft(a, n=None, axis=-1, norm=None, overwrite_input=False,
        planner_effort='FFTW_ESTIMATE', threads=1,
        auto_align_input=True, auto_contiguous=True)
```

has a `norm` argument, whereas 

```
def rfft(a, n=None, axis=-1, overwrite_input=False,
        planner_effort='FFTW_ESTIMATE', threads=1,
        auto_align_input=True, auto_contiguous=True):
```

does not.

The [numpy version](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/numpy/reference/generated/numpy.fft.rfft.html#numpy.fft.rfft) of the`rfft` method, does have a `norm` argument, as do all other numpy `*fft*` methods.

issue_title: sos remove command behavior
issue_body: Not sure if what other users think about it, but I'm getting negative feedback from my colleague who finds the remove feature confusing. It seems more intuitive to remove by default everything when one uses the system `rm` command. If one wants to keep the signature of certain steps then a version of `sos remove` can be used to make sure the signature is sufficient. I thought we had a reason not having adopted this design before but i cannot remember it. It was indeed inconvenient to keep track of all these at times, and I still often find myself doing `rm -r .sos` when things get messed up (fortunately a lot of my work does not need rerun once I harvest the results). Shall we rethink of the design of remove to see if we can make it more intuitive?

issue_title: The SoS Data Exchanger project
issue_body: Separate from #952 , this ticket keeps discussion on some thoughts on SoS Data Exchanger. The general idea is to use a neutral interface for all languages, with implementation details broken down to various functions that can be added incrementally in time. This is different design from using some intermediate common file formats, say JSON or HDF5, where there will be loss of information and performance issues.

> import zmq
zmq.\_\_version\_\_

'4.1.4'
context = zmq.Context.instance()
sock1 = context.socket(zmq.REP)
sock1.connect("inproc://mtest")
sock1.disconnect("inproc://mtest")
sock1.close()
sock2 = context.socket(zmq.REP)
sock2.bind("inproc://mtest")

```python
uids = db.get_uids(**search_params)

Would it be possible to support this? Or is there perhaps a better way?

thanks!

issue_title: allow sanitizing of custom types
issue_body:
Niket ran into an issue where he wants to include his own custom types in model output (and, in particular, in predictor output).

Unfortunately, there's no way to allow for this, except for something like manually doing serializing in `Model.decode`.

A somewhat simple fix would be to add another clause to `sanitize` that checks if an object has a `to_json()` method (or we could pick a different name) and calls it in that case rather than failing.

issue_title: Access denied when trying to access the pretrained decomposable attention model
Use masked_fill_ in replace_masked_values
issue_body:
I wrote this `replace_masked_values` function before I realized that [`masked_fill_`](http://pytorch.org/docs/master/tensors.html#torch.Tensor.masked_fill_) exists.  I'm pretty sure we could just use `masked_fill_` instead of what's currently here:

https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/blob/1700db4aa9323adb156cd87fd7bd9bba5b92532c/allennlp/nn/util.py#L463-L475

I'm not certain this works correctly with gradients and things, but it _should_...

issue_title: Arch linux install/usage instructions not correct
issue_body:
The [documentation page](https://certbot.eff.org/#arch-apache) for ArchLinux appears to be incorrect or outdated. The first step says to install the 'letsencrypt' package, but this doesn't exist in the Arch or AUR repositories:

```
> pacman -Ss letsencrypt
> packer -Ss letsencrypt
aur/letsencrypt.sh-git 0.2.0.r4.gd5b2858-1 (4)
    A Let's Encrypt (ACME protocol) client shell script
aur/letsencrypt-systemd-nginx 1.0-2 (2)
    Systemd service for Let's Encrypt certificate renewal with nginx servers
aur/ca-certificates-letsencrypt 20160325-1 (1)
    Let's Encrypt CA certificates
aur/letsencrypt-cli 0.3.0-1 (0)
    Yet another Letsencrypt (ACME) client using Ruby.
```

Several other matching options do show up in AUR. But as it stands, the instructions written on the doc page do not work so a first time user would have to some searching and then trial and error experimentation with these various clients, instead of having a straightforward documented user experience.

I did find a certbot package that is related to letsencrypt, although it is a different command binary than what listed in the doc page ("certbot" instead of "letsencrypt"). The command usage seems similar, though. Not sure if this should be the official client listed in the documentation instead?

issue_title: Check unchecked types in certbot-dns-rfc2136
issue_labels: [{'id': 277080619, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20code%20health', 'name': 'area: code health', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 263357278, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/area:%20testing', 'name': 'area: testing', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}, {'id': 149596829, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/certbot/certbot/labels/good%20first%20issue', 'name': 'good first issue', 'color': 'c61aff', 'default': True}]
issue_body:
This issue is for checking untyped definitions in the `certbot-dns-rfc2136` module.

To start, add the following to `mypy.ini` in the root of the repo:
```
[mypy-certbot_dns_rfc2136.*]
check_untyped_defs = True
```

Then run `tox -e mypy`. If passes, submit a PR. If not, fix errors and submit a PR when tests pass. You can read more about how all of this works below.

See explanation here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1fp0tPP9bzYkUViTnOXAyoT_KfgTfs8HSpNMoU8Tm_SY/edit?usp=sharing

From http://blog.zulip.org/2016/10/13/static-types-in-python-oh-mypy/:

Add the `--check-untyped-defs` option to your mypy arguments, and get that running with no errors on the codebase. This option causes mypy to check every def in the codebase for internal consistency; mypy can detect many classes of bugs and mistakes in your codebase, without your having written a single type annotation!

In most cases, you’ll want to fix bugs and bad code as you go, but you can also use #type: ignore annotations or exclude files to defer issues. For example, we excluded all of Zulip’s tests at first, since they’re both lower value to type-check and had most of the monkey-patching and other questionable Python in the project. For Zulip, getting clean `--check-untyped-defs` output took me about 2 days of hard work, including merging fixes for about 40 issues in the Zulip codebase.

I spent another day or two generating nice reproducers for the mypy bugs I’d encountered and improving typeshed. Now that mypy is no longer in its infancy, mypy bugs are increasingly rare. But a large project should expect to encounter and fix typeshed issues (just submit a PR!).

Once `mypy --check-untyped-defs` passes on your codebase, you should lock down your progress by running the mypy checker in your CI environment.

Because mypy type annotations are optional, once you’ve done the setup above, you can start annotating your codebase at whatever pace you like. Over time, you’ll get the benefits of static types in the parts of the codebase that you’ve annotated, without needing to do anything special to the rest of the codebase (the wonders of gradual typing!). In the next section, I’ll discuss strategy for how to move the codebase towards being fully annotated.

issue_title: forceMerge params are out of date
issue_body:
The [elasticsearch-py API docs](http://elasticsearch-py.readthedocs.io/en/master/api.html#elasticsearch.client.IndicesClient.forcemerge) state:

> Parameters:	
> index – A comma-separated list of index names; use _all or empty string to perform the operation on all indices
> allow_no_indices – Whether to ignore if a wildcard indices expression resolves into no concrete indices. (This includes _all string or when no indices have been specified)
> expand_wildcards – Whether to expand wildcard expression to concrete indices that are open, closed or both., default ‘open’, valid choices are: ‘open’, ‘closed’, ‘none’, ‘all’
> flush – Specify whether the index should be flushed after performing the operation (default: true)
ignore_unavailable – Whether specified concrete indices should be ignored when unavailable (missing or closed)
> max_num_segments – The number of segments the index should be merged into (default: dynamic)
> only_expunge_deletes – Specify whether the operation should only expunge deleted documents
operation_threading – TODO: ?
> wait_for_merge – Specify whether the request should block until the merge process is finished (default: true)

But the [Elasticsearch API docs](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/indices-forcemerge.html) state:

> **max_num_segments** The number of segments to merge to. To fully merge the index, set it to 1. Defaults to simply checking if a merge needs to execute, and if so, executes it.
>
> **only_expunge_deletes** Should the merge process only expunge segments with deletes in it. In Lucene, a document is not deleted from a segment, just marked as deleted. During a merge process of segments, a new segment is created that does not have those deletes. This flag allows to only merge segments that have deletes. Defaults to false. Note that this won’t override the index.merge.policy.expunge_deletes_allowed threshold.
>
> **flush** Should a flush be performed after the forced merge. Defaults to true.

A quick look showed that these 3 parameters have been the only ones acceptable since _Elasticsearch 1.5,_ when it was still called Optimize.

issue_title: Working with assignment/student from the command line
issue_body:
It used to be that nbgrader could automatically create assignments and students from running the various command line stages. That was removed, with the recommendation for instructors to edit their `nbgrader_config.py`. However, we are moving to a model where that file gets read only when JupyterHub itself is started (which would force the restarting of JupyterHub to add students/assignments) or when the single user notebook server is restarted (which would force that to be restarted).

I propose that for the upcoming release we add a lightweight command line way of managing assignments and students. Something like this:

```
$ nbgrader students add bsimpson --full-name "Bart Simpson"
# nbgrader students import students.csv # in some standard format
$ nbgrader assignment add assignment01 --due-date="..."
```

These commands shouldn't be too difficult and would relieve instructors from needing to work with the db through the Python API. This would really help the usability as we migrate first to formgrade being a service and then a serverextension.

@jhamrick

issue_title: File browser HTML view fails on spaces in directories
issue_body:
To reproduce:

1. create directory `/work/a space/`
2. create `/work/a space/index.html`
3. edit index.html -- editor launches correctly at `/edit/work/a%20space/index.html`
4. in file browser, select checkbox by index.html and hit view button
5. file browser incorrectly directs to `/view/work/a%2520space/index.html` and incorrectly displays a **404 : Not found**
6. manually correcting the path to `/view/work/a%20space/index.html` correctly displays the file

Summary: editing an HTML file on a spaced path works, viewing a file does not.

Note the %2520 -- this is a common double-encoding error, when the string is encoded once `a space` -> `a%20space` and then accidentally re-encoded `a%20space` -> `a%2520space`.

https://stackoverflow.com/a/16085190/7207622

issue_title: Rethink logging and make easier to override
issue_body:
I did the following for RaceBetter:

```python
import logging
from logging.config import dictConfig
from logging.handlers import RotatingFileHandler
import os.path as osp

import keg.logging
from pythonjsonlogger import jsonlogger


class Logging(keg.logging.Logging):

    def dict_config(self):
        log_config = self.config.get('LOGGING_CONFIG')
        if log_config:
            dictConfig(log_config)

    def init_app(self):
        super().init_app()
        self.dict_config()
        self.init_json_log()

    def init_json_log(self):
        log_fname = self.log_fname.replace('.', '_json.')
        log_fpath = osp.join(self.log_dpath, log_fname)

        log_max = self.config['KEG_LOG_MAX_BYTES']
        log_backups = self.config['KEG_LOG_MAX_BACKUPS']

        file_handler = RotatingFileHandler(log_fpath, maxBytes=log_max, backupCount=log_backups,
                                           encoding='utf-8')
        file_handler.setLevel(logging.INFO)

        include_fields = '%(asctime) %(pathname) %(funcName) %(lineno) %(message) %(levelname)' \
            ' %(name)s %(process) %(processName) %(message)'
        formatter = jsonlogger.JsonFormatter(include_fields)
        file_handler.setFormatter(formatter)

        for logger in self.loggers:
            logger.addHandler(file_handler)

        # Make the handler easier to get to by other code for custom logger/handler setups like
        # we did for the heartbeat CLI command.
        self.json_file_handler = file_handler
```

Seems like it could have been easier.  I also had to set `KEG_LOG_MAX_BYTES` to a really high level to turn off file rotation by Python so the system logrotate could handle it.

issue_title: match OIDC identity to Django user issues
issue_body:
Right now, mozilla-django-oidc matches an OIDC identity to a Django user via the Django user email field.

That's problematic for a couple of reasons:

1. the code is doing a case-sensitive exact match, so it's possible that could fail if the case ever changes (email addresses on some systems are case-insensitive)
2. the email field in the Django user table is not marked as unique and thus you can have multiple Django users with the same email field

I think to fix item 1, we want to do a case-insensitive match even though technically, email addresses are case-sensitive.

I think to fix item 2, we want to be using the username field of the Django user table and not the email field. Django 1.11's username field works fine with that. It means we don't have to generate usernames from email addresses. It means two users can't have the same email address. It solves a bunch of things for us.

Are there other issues we need to work around?

Are there better ways to solve this?

issue_title: How to run a simple RNN not LSTM?
Hide main functionality behind a flag
issue_body:
I'm debating whether it would be wise to require the detection to be behind a flag, the same way that after all is behind a flag, and that any other feature would also be behind a flag. Magic is best used sparingly and judiciously.

There are some places that I think magic is the only thing that makes sense. In particular, our hacks, of which there is currently only one, that allows the pypy3 pre-releases to work on Travis. There's no sense in doing that less magically.

The argument for the general detection behavior being behind a flag, however, is that it allows tox to run the same on Travis as it does locally. It's easy enough to add a flag in `tox_addoption`. Explicit opt-in allows users to decide exactly what they do (and don't) want overridden.

A future case where this might play out is if someone wishes to Tox-Travis for its ability to report back to GitHub (#73), but want to do all of that in a single job. It really shouldn't require a bunch of extra configuration work to do what Tox does by default.

An alternative would be a flag to _disable_ detection. That would be a backward compatible change, and would give the needed functionality, but it would have the flag being backward from rest of the features, which are (and are going to be) opt-in.

issue_title: Download and save Facebook images to our server
issue_body: When an event is submitted from a Facebook URL, instead of hot-linking directly to Facebook image URL upon save, we should download the image and save it to our server. This allows us to control caching, image optimization, and also prevents any broken links from occurring. Furthermore, it also provides consistency between user-uploaded images and Facebook images by making sure they are saved and accessed in the same way. To achieve this, we'll have to modify the way the event submission form works: - An additional **hidden** field should be added to the Event form, called `facebook_image_url`. This field will not be editable by the user, but will be pre-populated after data is fetched from the Facebook API. - If an image is retrieved from Facebook, the image upload field should hidden and replaced with a small preview of the Facebook image. This preview can be generated by simply adding an `` tag that links to the URL provided by the Facebook API. If the user wishes to replace the Facebook image, they can click "Upload image", which unhides the image upload field. - When performing an update, the form should first check to see if an image has been passed via the image upload field. If this is the case, that image should be processed and saved to the event model as it normally would - If no image is passed via the upload field, check to see if `facebook_image_url` contains a value. If this is the case, make a request to fetch the image and save it to our server/database (e.g. https://stackoverflow.com/a/1309682) - If both fields are empty, assume that no image was provided

issue_title: Implement scheduled restarts
issue_body:
Patroni can already schedule a switchover to be performed at a given date/time. We need to extend this mechanism to support restarts of a given node (most commonly master). The restarts should piggyback on our existing restart mechanism, which retains the role of a node being restarted.

The specific use-case we have for this is performing automatic minor version upgrades of our production databases during the off hours.

issue_title: Unused `response_factory` argument of `web.UrlDispatcher.add_static`/`web.StaticResource`
issue_body:
## Long story short

[Argument `response_factory`](http://aiohttp.readthedocs.io/en/v2.2.0/web_reference.html#aiohttp.web.UrlDispatcher.add_static) doesn't affect static responses

## Expected behaviour

aiohttp should call custom class passed to `aiohttp.web.UrlDispatcher.add_static()`

## Actual behaviour

`response_factory` value is ignored

## Steps to reproduce

```python
class CORSStreamResponse(web.StreamResponse):
    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
        if 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' not in self.headers:
            self.headers['Access-Control-Allow-Origin'] = '*'

# ...

app.router.add_static('/static', f'./static', name='static', response_factory=CORSStreamResponse)
```

```
$ curl -I http://localhost:8000/static/bundle.js
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
Content-Type: application/javascript
Last-Modified: Tue, 19 Sep 2017 10:08:45 GMT
Content-Length: 84833
Date: Mon, 25 Sep 2017 14:07:59 GMT
Server: Python/3.6 aiohttp/2.2.5
```

## Your environment

`aiohttp==2.2.5`

everywhere should be `except Exception`

issue_title: Expand concept of authentication
issue_body:
We have an internal use case where it is desirable for a process that runs as a different user from a user-owned watchman process to consume information from watchman.  The idea is that we don't want to have to commit multiple processes to watching the same tree(s).

In this scenario, the plan is to change the group of the unix socket and make it group writable so that this other process can connect.

This raises a security concern in that some of the watchman commands allow registering commands to be run as the owning user of the service.

To facilitate this use case, these things are needed:

1. Expand the various watchman clients so that they use SO_PEERCRED to transmit their credentials
2. Add a `CMD_REQUIRE_AUTH_OWNER` flag for the command dispatcher to have it check that the credentials of the client exactly match the owner and fail automatically if they don't.  We'd definitely want to set this for `trigger` and probably also for the `watch` and `watch-project` commands.
3. Add `sock_path_group` and `sock_path_mode` global configuration options that cause the listener to change the group and explicitly chmod the unix socket path after it has started up the listener
4. Add a global `require_unix_credentials` boolean that controls whether the listener will accept any commands from a client that hasn't passed in credentials.

The upshot of this is that it means that older clients that don't pass credentials won't be able to establish triggers; this is a behavior change, but it sounds reasonable.

Should also take a look at #204 and the proposed authenticate command in the protocol and see if that changes the outlook here.

Import serial breaks Python executable built with Nuitka
issue_body:
I'm attempting to build a very simple Python standalone executable using Nuitka:

    import serial
    print 'hello'

Building the executable using `nuitka --standalone --portable app.py` works, but if the PC's C:\Python27 directory is missing then the program fails with:

    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "C:\app\app.dist\app.py", line 6, in <module>
      File "C:\app\app.dist\serial\__init__.py", line 26, in serial
    
      File "C:\app\app.dist\serial\serialwin32.py", line 12, in serialwin32
      File "C:\Python27\lib\ctypes\__init__.py", line 10, in <module>
    
    ImportError: No module named _ctypes
    
I suppose technically this is an issue with Nuitka not properly finding dependencies (I will post there too), but many other modules do work. I haven't checked yet if serial/ctypes does some sort of tricky importing.

In the meantime the workaround on the pyserial side is to simply add `import _ctypes` at the top of `serialwin32.py`. If it helps I can make a pull request for this.

issue_title: How to statically compile example
issue_body:
I successfully got a working example to work from [your wiki](https://cmusphinx.github.io/wiki/tutorialpocketsphinx/#full-code-listing).

However, I am trying to package this as a statically linked executable.

Running,
```
gcc -o speechtotext speechtotext.c -DMODELDIR=\"`pkg-config --variable=modeldir pocketsphinx`\" `pkg-config --cflags --libs pocketsphinx sphinxbase`
```

produces a dynamically linked executable just fine. I am attempting to create the statically linked executable with the command:\
```
gcc -static -o speechtotext speechtotext.c -DMODELDIR=\"`pkg-config --variable=modeldir pocketsphinx`\" `pkg-config --cflags --libs pocketsphinx sphinxbase`
```

However, I am getting the error:
```
/usr/bin/ld: cannot find -lpulse
/usr/bin/ld: cannot find -lpulse-simple
```
Checking the result of the subcommand `pkg-config --cflags --libs pocketsphinx sphinxbase`, I see the result `-I/usr/local/include -I/usr/local/include/sphinxbase -I/usr/local/include/pocketsphinx -I/usr/local/include -I/usr/local/include/sphinxbase -L/usr/local/lib -lpocketsphinx -lsphinxbase -lsphinxad -lpulse -lpulse-simple -lpthread -lm`.

I notice that by default, the debian installtion of `libpulse-dev` installs the libraries `libpulse.so` and `libpulse-simple.so` to `/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/`. Hence I tried to symlink these into `/usr/local/lib` where the `pkg-config` command looks for the `-lpocketsphinx -lsphinxbase -lsphinxad` etc.

However, I get the exact same error. Any idea why this is happening? Would this be something wrong with the way I am trying to statically link? Or is an executable relying on `libpulse` fundamentally unable to statically compile for some reason?

Any help would be much appreciated

issue_title: Flask extension does not load configuration lazily
issue_body:
I am using `raven[flask]==6.1.0` and it appears that the flask extension loads the configuration as soon as possible, rather than using lazy evaluation as Flask recommends.
This behavior results in no exception being forwarded to sentry, since my project needs to load the configuration after the application has been created.

I attach a snippet that replicates the issue.
```python
import mock
import pytest

import flask
from raven.contrib.flask import Sentry

@mock.patch('raven.base.Client.send', autospect=True, return_value=None)
def test_lazy_configuration(m):
    app = flask.Flask(__name__)
    @app.route('/')
    def index():
        raise ValueError('Error here')

    app.sentry = Sentry(app)
    app.config['SENTRY_CONFIG'] = {
        'dsn': 'https://user:pass@whatever.com/44',
    }

    response = app.test_client().get('/')
    m.assert_called_once()
```

issue_title: Standarize testing patterns
issue_labels: [{'id': 324324196, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/F5Networks/f5-common-python/labels/critical', 'name': 'critical', 'color': '006b75', 'default': False}, {'id': 375204748, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/F5Networks/f5-common-python/labels/DESIGN', 'name': 'DESIGN', 'color': '5319e7', 'default': False}, {'id': 278738625, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/F5Networks/f5-common-python/labels/help%20wanted', 'name': 'help wanted', 'color': '159818', 'default': True}, {'id': 278738627, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/F5Networks/f5-common-python/labels/question', 'name': 'question', 'color': 'cc317c', 'default': True}, {'id': 301394552, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/F5Networks/f5-common-python/labels/unit%20test', 'name': 'unit test', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
As per comment in PR #1040, I would like us to discuss the established patterns for testing in this library, these will include:

- What we test in unit and functional tests, mainly the scope of those tests.
- The format of test fixtures, e.g out with teardown() in with yield
- Factory classes to reduce the amount of repetitive code.
- When not to test certain elements, e.g due to specifics of BIGIP operation, or limitations, whether its 
feasible to work around those.
- Adherence to code coverage, e.g. no 100% code coverage (whenever possible) means no PR approval

@caphrim007 @jasonrahm @zancas @pjbreaux

issue_title: Connection heartbeat arg description doesn't match implementation
issue_body:
The `heartbeat` arg description is 
> ``heartbeat`` Default None (disabled). If 0, broker assigned. If >0, negotiated with broker.

The implementation appears to be that if user passed `heartbeat=None`, then the heartbeat value is broker-assigned; if user passed `heartbeat=0`, then heartbeats would be disabled. The quoted description is the reverse of what actually happens in the code.

```
        if self.connection._heartbeat is None:

```

issue_title: Add a way to provide default ordering for the table with a message
issue_body:
A listing typically has a default ordering, and this typically means something important. One example is blogposts which is ordered by creation time, but also has an optional sort index that can be set to force the blogpost to the top. You will most likely want ``(sort_index, creationtime)`` to be the default sort order of the listview, and if you want users to understand this you will most likely need an information box above the table. I think it should be possible to define this default sort order (or get it from ``model._meta.ordering``), and it should be possible to define two messages:

``default_sortorder_message_template``
 > An include template that we render when the default sort order is applied. This will typically tell admins that the list is ordered as it is on the website.

``non_default_sortorder_message_template``
 > An include template that we render when any sort order other than the default is applied. This will typically have a link to reset the sortorder to the default.

issue_title: Out of date python libraries in apt
issue_body:
Unfortunately, it looks like there are some out-of-date python packages in `apt` which affect blockstack-core.

This will be an issue for people trying to run `blockstack-core` with browser via our apt repository (or the docker containers built using that repository).

In particular, blockstack-core depends on python-cryptography >= 2.0 (not in apt) and also python-requests >= 1.10 (not in xenial, I think)

It's not obvious to me how to solve this problem. For the docker containers, shipping them with `pip` builds is simple enough. But if someone wants to run blockstack-core outside of a container, there'll be issues...

Adding @jackzampolin for input here. Maybe we just move to install either from docker or (for devs) from pip?
